title: Integer overflow when converting ULONG to jsize
problem: cipher_len is ULONG (unsigned long) but is cast to jsize (signed int). If cipher_len exceeds INT_MAX, it will overflow and cause a negative value, potentially leading to buffer allocation issues.
code:
jsize ctm_len = (jsize)native_cipher->L1;
    jbyteArray ctm_array = (*env)->NewByteArray(env, ctm_len);

title: Getter returns direct reference to internal array allowing modification
problem: The getX() and getY() methods return direct references to internal arrays instead of defensive copies. This allows callers to modify the internal state of cryptographic objects, breaking encapsulation and potentially causing security vulnerabilities.

title: Getter returns direct reference to internal array allowing modification
problem: The getK() method returns a direct reference to the private key array, allowing callers to modify the key material.

title: Getter returns direct reference to internal array allowing modification
problem: The getR() and getS() methods return direct references to internal arrays, allowing callers to modify signature values.

title: Getter returns direct reference to internal array allowing modification
problem: The getX(), getY(), getM(), and getC() methods return direct references to internal arrays, allowing callers to modify ciphertext data.

title: Getter returns direct reference to internal array allowing modification
problem: The getM() and getE() methods return direct references to internal arrays, allowing callers to modify public key material.

title: Getter returns direct reference to internal array allowing modification
problem: The getM(), getE(), getD(), getPrime(), getPexp(), and getCoef() methods return direct references to internal arrays, exposing private key material to modification.

title: Missing null validation in setters for sensitive key material
problem: The setM(), setE(), setD(), setPrime(), setPexp(), and setCoef() methods do not validate null input, potentially allowing null key material to be set.

title: Missing null validation in setters for key material
problem: The setM() and setE() methods do not validate null input.

title: Direct array exposure for algorithm abilities
problem: getAsymAlgAbility() returns direct reference to internal array.

title: Resource leak - sponsor_id_buf not released on error path
problem: If java_to_native_ECCPublicKey fails after allocating sponsor_id_buf, the buffer is not released.

title: Validation uses stale l1 value in setCtM
problem: When setCtM is called, it checks this.l1 against the new array, but this.l1 may have been set previously. The check should happen after the new l1 is set, or validation should be coordinated.

title: Validation uses stale l value in setSigM
problem: When setSigM is called, it checks this.l which may be stale from previous usage.

title: Direct reference to sensitive encrypted key material
problem: getEncryptedKey() returns direct reference to internal array containing encrypted key material.

title: Direct reference to sensitive ciphertext data
problem: getCtM() returns direct reference to internal array containing PQC ciphertext.

title: Direct reference to signature data
problem: getSigM() returns direct reference to internal array containing signature data.

title: Missing null check in asymAlgAbility setter
problem: The setAsymAlgAbility() method checks for null but getAsymAlgAbility() returns the array directly without null check, potentially causing NPE when accessed via default constructor.

title: Constructor accepts 2D array without proper validation
problem: The constructor accepts prime and pexp arrays without validating they have exactly 2 elements or that each element has correct length.

title: Resource leak - data_buf not released on error path
problem: If java_to_native_ECCSignature fails after data_buf is allocated, data_buf is not released.

title: Inconsistent validation - allows zero-length cipher
problem: The validation allows l to be 0 even when c is non-empty, which is semantically incorrect.

title: Missing validation for sig_m_len in native_to_java_HybridSignature
problem: sig_m_len is not validated against HYBRIDSIGref_MAX_LEN before being used for array creation.

title: Removed upper bound validation for bits field
problem: The setBits() method no longer validates that bits <= RSA_MAX_BITS, allowing invalid key sizes to be set.

title: Removed upper bound validation for bits field
problem: The setBits() method no longer validates that bits <= ECC_MAX_BITS (512).

title: Inconsistent error handling order - critical conversions happen before memory allocation
problem: Converting responsePublicKey/responseTmpPublicKey should happen BEFORE allocating critical memory (response_id_buf) to avoid needing to release on error.
